project.name: orbit-wars
project.description: Versioned Orbit Wars reinforcement-learning tutorial implementation

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything exclusion patterns scoped to Orbit Wars project sources, omitting venv, OMG/Cursor tooling, training outputs, and MCP server from graph analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors .gitignore-style globs with explicit negation for forcing includes under default excludes.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Incremental analysis fingerprint store mapping project file paths to content hashes, version, and git commit for change detection across rebuilds.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Lightweight metadata recording last analysis timestamp, git commit, schema version, and analyzed file count for the Understand-Anything workspace.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md

nodes.name: config.yaml

nodes.languageNotes: Uses Hydra defaults list and custom resolvers such as orbit_run_id and orbit_safe_rel for output layout.

nodes.name: default.yaml

nodes.name: 4p_16env.yaml
nodes.summary: 4-player-only format schedule weighting four-player games and allocating 16 four-player envs.

nodes.name: 4p_32env.yaml
nodes.summary: 4-player-only format schedule with 32 four-player parallel environments and no two-player rollouts.

nodes.name: empty.yaml

nodes.summary: Mixed 2p/4p format profile balancing player counts with 16-env rollout groups for each format.

layers.description: Pytest coverage validating Hydra config consolidation, JAX env/policy/PPO parity, features, curriculum, telemetry, artifacts, and submission packaging.

tour.title: Hydra Training Entry
tour.description: Start where README.md points newcomers: `uv run python -m src.train` is the canonical launch path. This thin Hydra CLI resolves overrides from `conf/` responsibility groups (model, task, reward, training, format, curriculum, opponents, telemetry, artifacts) into a typed `TrainConfig`, then hands execution to the JAX stack. Treat it as the map of how experiments are named and started—not where learning happens.
tour.languageLesson: Hydra composes YAML groups at runtime; CLI overrides like `model=attention training.total_updates=1000` map to nested config without editing defaults on disk.

tour.title: Config Schema & Runtime
tour.description: Building on the entrypoint, `src/config/schema.py` defines the dataclass contract every responsibility group must satisfy—player counts, rollout groups, PPO hyperparameters, and artifact cadence. `src/config/runtime.py` registers OmegaConf resolvers, composes Hydra output, and validates curriculum and telemetry settings before training starts. Together they are the bridge between `conf/*.yaml` files and the objects `src/jax/train.py` consumes.

tour.title: JAX Training Orchestrator
tour.description: After configuration is resolved, `src/jax/train.py` becomes the real control plane: it selects devices, builds vectorized environments, runs the PPO update loop, advances curriculum stages, logs metrics, and schedules checkpoint/artifact work. Follow this file to see how a Hydra run becomes thousands of batched JAX steps rather than reading game rules directly.

tour.title: Game Rules & Reference Env
tour.description: Orbit Wars mechanics live under `src/game/`: shared limits in `constants.py`, structured state in `types.py`, and a readable Python `env.py` that implements reset/step, opponent hooks, and reward shaping. The JAX trainer mirrors this semantics; parity tests treat this package as ground truth when batched code diverges.

tour.title: Observation Features
tour.description: Policies never see raw planets directly—`src/features/registry.py` fixes ordered feature slices and dimensions, `encoding.py` builds self/candidate/global tensors with history on the Python path, and `src/jax/features.py` reproduces the same layout for JIT rollouts. Changes here ripple through policy input shapes, checkpoint compatibility checks, and both Python and JAX tests.

tour.title: JAX Environment & PPO
tour.description: The accelerated loop pairs `src/jax/env.py`—batched stepping, combat, mixed 2p/4p formats—with `src/jax/ppo.py`, which collects rollouts, applies trajectory shields, and performs PPO updates. This is the heart of training throughput: environment transitions supply rewards and masks; PPO turns trajectories into gradient steps for the policy modules configured via Hydra `model=`.
tour.languageLesson: JAX `jit`/`vmap` batch independent envs on device; PPO reuses the same static shapes defined by task config (players, candidates, history frames) to avoid recompilation.

tour.title: Policy Architectures
tour.description: `src/jax/policy.py` implements Flax modules (MLP, attention, entity transformer, GNN pointer) with shared value heads and action masking hooks. Hydra `conf/model/*.yaml` selects architecture presets; this file shows how observations flow through encoders to action logits and value estimates used in `ppo.py`.

tour.title: Safe Launches & Opponents
tour.description: Gameplay safety and training diversity meet in `src/game/trajectory_shield.py`, which blocks illegal fleet launches during rollouts, while `src/opponents/pool.py` and `runtime.py` mix random, scripted, self-play, and snapshot opponents. `src/training/curriculum.py` promotes formats and opponent families using rolling win-rate gates tied to Hydra `curriculum=` and `opponents=` groups.

tour.title: Telemetry & Experiment Logging
tour.description: Training visibility is centralized in `src/telemetry/metric_registry.py`, which names metric groups toggled by `telemetry=` configs, and `logger.py`, which flattens rollout/PPO scalars for W&B or JSONL. These modules decouple `jax/train.py` from any one backend so throughput-only runs can drop heavy diagnostic groups without touching the learner.

tour.title: Checkpoints & Artifact Pipeline
tour.description: Long runs finish in `src/artifacts/`: `pipeline.py` queues async checkpoint and replay jobs, `replay.py` evaluates saved policies against configured opponents, and `checkpoint_compat.py` rejects stale checkpoints when feature schemas or config ownership change. `run_paths.py` (pulled in by the orchestrator) ties Hydra output directories to manifests—follow these when debugging missing checkpoints after a config migration.
